archived_by: PM (V1.49 P-last T1 confirmed pre-existing clippy drift was machine-specific, not V1.49 regression)

residual_findings.title: Pre-existing cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings failure on clean V1.49 integration HEAD (local toolchain drift)
residual_findings.source: P0 fix-wave implementer (out-of-scope finding); verified pre-existing per .mstar/AGENTS.md protocol; closed in V1.49 P-last T1
residual_findings.scope: crates/nexus-contracts/src/local/schedule/http.rs:236 (redundant_clone); crates/nexus-orchestration + crates/nexus-daemon-runtime test targets (doc_markdown, unwrap_used). 131 errors on clean base, identical count in W-1 fix worktree (clippy-neutral diff).
residual_findings.closure_note: V1.49 P-last T1 fix wave (commit fb52ea11 merge at 60311a9b): cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings verified CLEAN on iteration/v1.49 @ 60311a9b. The 'pre-existing clippy failure' claim by the V1.49 P0 fix-wave implementer was a toolchain-specific drift (local clippy rust-1.93.0 stricter than the clippy used by CI/qc3). Not a V1.49 code regression. Independently verified by V1.49 P0 QC3 (commit ecabd0ac), P1 QC3 (commit 990a63b6), P2 QC3 (commit 4632c87b), and P3 QC3 (commit 115e94ac) — all reported cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings clean.
residual_findings.closure_evidence: iteration/v1.49 @ 60311a9b (P-last T1 merge) + fb52ea11 (fmt commit); 4 independent QC3 verifications of cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings clean on V1.49 integration HEAD
